questions.question: What did the U.S. do after JD Vance failed to secure a peace deal with Iran in Islamabad?
options: Began a naval blockade of ships entering or leaving Iranian ports
Withdrew all U.S. forces from the Persian Gulf
Recognized Iran's right to enrich uranium for 20 years
Opened direct trade talks with Tehran
questions.explanation: The U.S. began a naval blockade of ships entering or leaving Iranian ports after the talks failed. Trump said the blockade was meant to stop what he called Iranian "extortion."

questions.question: Who did Trump call "WEAK on Crime" and "terrible for Foreign Policy" after criticism of the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran?
questions.explanation: Trump attacked Pope Leo XIV after the pope condemned the war and said military action would not create space for freedom or peace. Leo responded that he had "no fear" of the Trump administration.

questions.question: What did the divided federal appeals court do in the fight over deportation flights carrying Venezuelan migrants to El Salvador?
options: Ordered the flights to return to the U.S. immediately
Upheld criminal contempt charges against Trump officials
Shut down Judge James Boasberg's contempt investigation
Referred the case directly to the Supreme Court
questions.explanation: The 2-1 D.C. Circuit ruling ended Boasberg's contempt investigation for now, calling it an abuse of discretion. The majority said his earlier order was not clear enough to support criminal contempt.

questions.question: Who was Trump's nominee to replace Jerome Powell as Federal Reserve chair who disclosed financial holdings worth well over $100 million?
questions.explanation: Kevin Warsh's Senate ethics filing showed holdings worth well over $100 million. The filing also said he received about $10 million in consulting fees from Stanley Druckenmiller's family office.

questions.question: Which senator was the only Republican to support the Democratic resolution to force Trump to end U.S. military action in Iran without congressional approval?
questions.explanation: Rand Paul was the only Republican to back the measure, while John Fetterman was the only Democrat to oppose it. Senate Republicans blocked the resolution for the fourth time.

questions.question: What change were lawmakers demanding before renewing Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act?
options: A ban on all foreign intelligence collection during election years
A requirement that Congress approve each surveillance request individually
A warrant before agencies can search Americans' communications or buy personal data from brokers
An end to all intelligence sharing with allied governments
questions.explanation: A bipartisan group wanted privacy changes, including a warrant requirement before agencies search Americans' communications or buy personal data from brokers. Opponents argued that requirement would make Section 702 harder to use quickly.

questions.question: During a Pentagon prayer service, Pete Hegseth recited a fake prayer inspired by which movie?
questions.explanation: Hegseth read a made-up verse the Pentagon later said was "obviously inspired" by Pulp Fiction. He referred to it as "CSAR 25:17," echoing the film's fictional version of Ezekiel 25:17.

questions.question: How many Haitians in the U.S. would have Temporary Protected Status restored for three years under the bipartisan bill passed by the House?
questions.explanation: The House passed a bill to restore Temporary Protected Status for about 350,000 Haitians for three years. The measure would also reinstate work permits and deportation protections the administration had tried to end.
